triggers.name: cron
triggers.notes: 5-field crontab or 6-field (with seconds); re-read on change, no restart needed

triggers.name: webhook
triggers.notes: POST /hooks/{path}; body = trigger payload; hmac_secret_env (optional) enforces X-Hub-Signature-256

triggers.name: poll
triggers.syntax: poll: { interval: 5m, http: {method, url, headers}, extract: {jsonpath|css}, fire_when: "changed" | expression }
triggers.notes: first poll runs immediately, then every interval; "changed" compares the (extracted) value hash; expressions fire on false→true edges

triggers.name: rss
triggers.notes: one execution per NEW item (guid/link dedupe, persisted); first poll baselines silently

stepFields.notes: unique step id; outputs visible as steps.<name>.*
http | telegram | warsaw_waste | ssl_check | your own ActionHandler bean
action inputs; values support {{ }} templating and ${ENV}
condition; false → step SKIPPED (counts as satisfied for dependents)
[a, b] — runs when ALL succeeded; omitted = previous step; [] = root; independent steps run in parallel
{ max_attempts: 3, base_delay: 10s, max_delay: 10m } — exponential backoff, full jitter; exhausted → DLQ

conditions.grammar: expr := or; or := and ('||' and)*; and := unit ('&&' unit)*; unit := '(' expr ')' | a OP b | contains(x, y) | exists(path) | path
conditions.functions: contains(haystack, needle) — substring or list membership; exists(path) — non-null
conditions.validated: syntax checked at create/update — malformed conditions are rejected with 400

templating.notes: webhook payload / poll response / rss item; poll extract value = trigger.value
outputs of steps listed (transitively) in needs
environment variable, resolved at execution time, never stored

actions.name: http
actions.output: {status, headers, body} — body parsed as JSON when possible; fail_on_status: false records non-2xx as success

actions.name: warsaw_waste
actions.output: {tomorrow_date, tomorrow[], tomorrow_count, summary, upcoming[]} — Warsaw waste schedule

actions.name: ssl_check
actions.output: {host, port, days_left, not_after, subject, issuer} — reads the served TLS certificate, even expired ones
